name: Transparent Hands USA
mission: Conduct free life-saving surgeries/medical treatments for poor people all across Pakistan through its transparent crowdfunding online healthcare platform.

scoreSummary: Transparent Hands Foundation (US) INC shows strong alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: A review for Transparent Hands Foundation US Inc. states, "i was able to give zakat easily to desired and eligible patients." Additionally, Transparent Hands Foundation (US) Inc. is listed on LaunchGood with "Zakat" as a giving option. Transparent Hands is also described as a "Shariah-compliant organization and thus you can pay your Zakat, Sadaqah, or Ramadan donations with complete confidence." Furthermore, Transparent Hands USA, a 501(c)3 organization, explicitly states, "Transparent Hands allows users from all over the world to donate Zakat, Sadaqah, and other forms of donations to needy patients in Pakistan while ensuring complete transparency."

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 6.9% of revenue ($42,000 on $606,605)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

score_details.score_summary: Transparent Hands Foundation (US) INC shows strong alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance.

zakat.claim_evidence: A review for Transparent Hands Foundation US Inc. states, "i was able to give zakat easily to desired and eligible patients." Additionally, Transparent Hands Foundation (US) Inc. is listed on LaunchGood with "Zakat" as a giving option. Transparent Hands is also described as a "Shariah-compliant organization and thus you can pay your Zakat, Sadaqah, or Ramadan donations with complete confidence." Furthermore, Transparent Hands USA, a 501(c)3 organization, explicitly states, "Transparent Hands allows users from all over the world to donate Zakat, Sadaqah, and other forms of donations to needy patients in Pakistan while ensuring complete transparency."

all_citations.claim: The charity has a score of 89.75/100 and a program expense ratio of 72.0%.
The organization reported total revenue of $606,605.
The charity is Zakat-eligible and maintains specific Islamic giving policies.
The organization provides dedicated options for Zakat donations.
The foundation provides free consultations and screenings for blood pressure, diabetes, and Hepatitis B & C.
The organization organizes medical camps to serve patients in Pakistan.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 57/100, driven by its high alignment with humanitarian causes and transparent financial reporting, though its overall impact score is tempered by limited liquidity.
areas_for_improvement: Limited working capital of 1.0 months, which may impact long-term financial sustainability.
Moderate impact score due to the lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data in public filings.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 36/50, the charity shows a high fit for donors interested in humanitarian medical relief and Islamic giving, as it is officially Zakat-eligible [3][4]. Its mission addresses urgent healthcare gaps in Pakistan through a network of partner hospitals [5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates direct service effectiveness by organizing medical camps that provide free consultations and screenings for Hepatitis B & C and diabetes [5][6]. While it maintains a high program expense ratio of 72.0%, the impact score of 21/50 reflects a need for more granular data on long-term patient outcomes and cost-efficiency per procedure [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Transparent Hands Foundation (US) INC provides life-saving surgical and medical treatments to underprivileged patients in Pakistan through a transparent crowdfunding platform.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 72.0%, ensuring the majority of funds go directly to medical services.
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.22 to raise every $1.
Zakat-eligible status with clear policies for Islamic giving.
baseline_narrative.summary: Transparent Hands Foundation (US) INC works with numerous hospitals across Pakistan to provide free surgeries and medical procedures to those in need [5]. The organization reported a total revenue of $606,605 and maintains a Charity Navigator score of 89.75/100, reflecting strong accountability and financial health [1][2].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 57/100, driven by its high alignment with humanitarian causes and transparent financial reporting, though its overall impact score is tempered by limited liquidity.

areas_for_improvement.context: The charity currently holds only <cite id="1">1.0 months of working capital</cite>, which is significantly below the recommended 3-12 month buffer for financial sustainability.
While the charity tracks patient numbers, it currently lacks <cite id="1">specific cost-per-beneficiary data</cite> in public filings, making it difficult to assess relative cost-effectiveness.

bbb_assessment.summary: The charity provides audited financial statements but has not been fully vetted against all 20 BBB standards.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization is actively seeking to diversify its donor base through its global crowdfunding platform.
risk_factors: <cite id="1">Working capital of only 1.0 months</cite> creates risk during funding dips
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve financial volatility and governance scale: <cite id="1">revenue has declined by -11.9%</cite> over the last three years, and the <cite id="1">board size is minimal</cite>.

confidence.notes: Strong alignment between third-party ratings and internal audits.

alignment.explanation: The charity is a strong fit for Muslim donors, as it is <cite id="3">zakat-eligible</cite> and serves the <cite id="3">masakin (needy)</cite> in a Muslim-majority region.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be strengthened by <cite id="1">expanding services to more underserved geographies</cite> within Pakistan that have limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this organization due to its <cite id="1">89/100 Accountability score</cite> and its status as a Platinum-level participant on Candid. The charity also undergoes <cite id="6">independent financial audits</cite> to ensure fiscal integrity.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization should <cite id="1">expand its board of directors</cite> beyond the current 3 members to ensure more diverse oversight.

impact.explanation: The charity demonstrates impact through <cite id="1">direct service delivery</cite> and a <cite id="1">72.0% program expense ratio</cite>, which is respectable for its size, though lower than the <cite id="1">peer median of 88.2%</cite>.
impact.improvement: The foundation could improve its impact score by <cite id="1">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for a formal cost-effectiveness analysis.

rich_narrative.headline: Transparent Hands Foundation (US) INC provides life-saving surgical and medical treatments to underprivileged patients in Pakistan through a transparent crowdfunding platform.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to provide direct, life-saving medical interventions in Pakistan and value seeing the specific individual their contribution helps.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations through healthcare
Value transparency and seeing specific patient stories
Prefer direct service delivery over advocacy
Comfortable supporting a lean, growth-stage organization
giving_considerations: The organization operates with very low cash reserves
Revenue has shown a downward trend recently
Impact is highly localized to Pakistan
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large financial endowments or those looking for broad systemic healthcare policy reform.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes and direct service, but lacks a rigorous external evaluation or cost-per-beneficiary analysis.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The charity provides a platform to fund surgeries for those in poverty, leading to restored health and economic productivity.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that medical interventions are reaching the most deserving patients effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a significant funding gap and high demand for surgeries in Pakistan, the charity can absorb more capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Unlike larger peers, Transparent Hands uses a <cite id="5">crowdfunding model</cite> that allows for patient-specific transparency.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 72.0%</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of funds are spent on direct medical services rather than administration.
Transparent Hands is highly efficient in its resource mobilization, spending only <cite id="1">$0.23 to raise every $1</cite> in total contributions.
The charity has earned a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 score for Culture & Accountability</cite> from Charity Navigator and holds a Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid.

strengths_deep_dive: Transparent Hands excels in its commitment to governance and leadership, achieving <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 scores in both Culture and Leadership</cite> categories. This reflects a robust internal structure and a clear strategic vision for expanding healthcare access. Their operational model is verified as <cite id="1">direct service delivery</cite>, which minimizes intermediary costs. Furthermore, the organization has demonstrated consistent output in its community outreach, maintaining a steady track record of <cite id="7">60 medical camps annually</cite> to reach rural populations in Pakistan.
rich_narrative.summary: Transparent Hands Foundation (US) INC operates a specialized crowdfunding platform designed to bridge the healthcare gap for underprivileged patients in Pakistan. The organization <cite id="5">works with numerous hospitals across Pakistan</cite> to facilitate free surgeries and medical procedures for those who cannot afford them. In the 2023 fiscal year, the charity reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $584,685</cite>, primarily driven by public contributions. The organization maintains a <cite id="1">Charity Navigator score of 89.75/100</cite>, which signals a high level of accountability and transparency. Beyond individual surgeries, the foundation conducts large-scale outreach, having <cite id="6">organized 60 medical camps</cite> in a single year to provide diagnostic services. These camps typically serve <cite id="7">between 2,500 and 3,000 patients per camp</cite>, offering free consultations and screenings for diabetes and hepatitis. By leveraging a digital platform, the charity allows donors to see the specific patients they are supporting, creating a direct link between contribution and clinical outcome.
